before.name: cusi_case
before.comment: A table used to store suggested investigations to be shown to a checkuser. Used by the Suggested investigations feature of the CheckUser extension.

columns.name: sic_url_identifier
columns.comment: A randomised ID which is used in a hexadecimal format to provide a URL to the case. Using a random number avoids exposing how many cases exist or when the case was created when it is included in a URL.

columns.name: sic_status
columns.comment: Integer acting as an enum, which represents the status of this case. Open is 0, Closed as resolved is 1, Closed as invalid is 2

columns.name: sic_status_reason
columns.comment: The reason given for changing sic_status to either closed as resolved (1) or closed as invalid (2).

columns.name: sic_created_timestamp
columns.comment: The timestamp representing when the case was created by the system.

columns.name: sic_updated_timestamp
columns.comment: The timestamp representing when the case last had a signal/user added by the system (or the created timestamp if no users/signals have been added since it's creation).

indexes.name: sic_status_updated_timestamp_id
indexes.comment: Used for several things. Allows sorting and paging the Codex table by the 'Status' column (the updated timestamp and ID are used to ensure unique paging). Also allows the system to find the newest open case given a pre-determined list of sic_id values, so the system can add new users to an existing case.

indexes.name: sic_updated_timestamp_id
indexes.comment: Used to sort and page cases by their updated timestamp (the ID makes paging consistent in case two cases have the same updated timestamp) for the Codex table on the Special page for Suggested investigations.

indexes.name: sic_url_identifier
indexes.comment: Allows searching for cases based on their URL identifier (such as when a user loads the special page for a given case using this identifier). Secondly, allows the system to check and enforce that the URL identifier is unique for each case.

after.name: cusi_case
after.comment: A table used to store suggested investigations to be shown to a checkuser. Used by the Suggested investigations feature of the CheckUser extension.

columns.name: sic_url_identifier
columns.comment: A randomised ID which is used in a hexadecimal format to provide a URL to the case. Using a random number avoids exposing how many cases exist or when the case was created when it is included in a URL.

columns.name: sic_status
columns.comment: Integer acting as an enum, which represents the status of this case. Open is 0, Closed as resolved is 1, Closed as invalid is 2

columns.name: sic_status_reason
columns.comment: The reason given for changing sic_status to either closed as resolved (1) or closed as invalid (2).

columns.name: sic_status_changed_by
columns.comment: The user ID of the user who last changed the status. 0 for system actions (e.g. auto-close). NULL when status change tracking data is not available.

columns.name: sic_created_timestamp
columns.comment: The timestamp representing when the case was created by the system.

columns.name: sic_updated_timestamp
columns.comment: The timestamp representing when the case last had a signal/user added by the system (or the created timestamp if no users/signals have been added since it's creation).

indexes.name: sic_status_updated_timestamp_id
indexes.comment: Used for several things. Allows sorting and paging the Codex table by the 'Status' column (the updated timestamp and ID are used to ensure unique paging). Also allows the system to find the newest open case given a pre-determined list of sic_id values, so the system can add new users to an existing case.

indexes.name: sic_updated_timestamp_id
indexes.comment: Used to sort and page cases by their updated timestamp (the ID makes paging consistent in case two cases have the same updated timestamp) for the Codex table on the Special page for Suggested investigations.

indexes.name: sic_url_identifier
indexes.comment: Allows searching for cases based on their URL identifier (such as when a user loads the special page for a given case using this identifier). Secondly, allows the system to check and enforce that the URL identifier is unique for each case.

indexes.name: sic_status_changed_by_updated_timestamp_id
indexes.comment: Allows filtering cases by the user who last changed the status and sorting the filtered results by updated timestamp.
